_notes: Block 1 default values per region. Labels updated in v5 per audits/final_consistency/V5_MITIGATION_DEFAULT_PROVENANCE_FIX.md. Only BEV-share growth and low-carbon electricity growth are framed as policy-derived. CAV and STI targets are baseline scenario assumptions, not official state targets. Hardware doubling time is industry consensus, not state specific.

_sources.cav_target_2075: Baseline scenario assumption. No California statute sets a CAV fleet share by 2075. CARB's AV testing framework regulates testing and deployment permits and does not project fleet share. This default is an illustrative scenario number aligned with California's demonstrated regulatory willingness to enable CAV deployment.
_sources.sti_target_2075: Baseline scenario assumption. Caltrans operates smart-corridor and connected-intersection pilots but has not adopted a statewide coverage target for 2075. This default reflects a projection consistent with current pilot-scale deployment extrapolation.
_sources.bev_growth_rate: Policy derived. CARB Advanced Clean Cars II mandates 100% zero-emission-vehicle sales by 2035; DOE AFDC reports a 2019 to 2024 California light-duty BEV registration compound annual growth rate near 45% from a low base. The default 0.07 annual share growth projects a decline from the recent CAGR toward saturation at a mid-century ZEV fleet.
_sources.low_carbon_electricity_growth: Policy derived. California SB 100 mandates 100% clean electricity by 2045. Starting from a 65.6% low-carbon share in 2024, an annual increment of 0.05 is consistent with the SB 100 trajectory.
_sources.hardware_doubling_years: Industry consensus. Not California specific. Years required for average ECAV computing energy per vehicle to halve based on semiconductor-efficiency roadmap projections and published AV-compute benchmarks.
_sources.hardware_deployment_lag_years: Expert prior / scenario assumption. Frontier compute-efficiency gains are not realized instantly in operating ATS fleets because qualification, integration, procurement, installation, and fleet turnover each take time. Default 2.0 years; epistemic prior U(1, 4) years.

_sources.cav_target_2075: Conservative baseline scenario assumption. Ohio has no statewide CAV mandate and ODOT has funded only pilot-scale testing. In the absence of an enabling policy, 0.25 is a defensibly conservative 2075 ceiling that sits below California's optimistic 0.45. Reviewers who want to test higher adoption should move the slider explicitly.
_sources.sti_target_2075: Conservative baseline scenario assumption. Ohio TSMO supports smart-signal deployment on selected corridors but has no statewide 2075 coverage target. 0.30 is a conservative extrapolation of the current programme and does not claim policy derivation.
_sources.bev_growth_rate: Conservative empirical. Ohio has no zero-emission-vehicle mandate. AFDC 2019 to 2024 Ohio BEV registrations show a 30 % compound annual growth rate from a 0.7 % base. A 0.03 annual compound growth in fleet share is a defensibly slow long-horizon attenuation of the short-horizon AFDC signal and is the value we revert to in v5.1. Higher values are not supported by a state mandate.
_sources.low_carbon_electricity_growth: Conservative empirical. Ohio's combined coal and gas share is near 75 % (EIA 2024). Ohio has no statewide clean-electricity mandate. 0.02 annual compound growth from the 24.7 % baseline is the defensibly slow scenario the literature supports and is the value we revert to in v5.1.
_sources.hardware_doubling_years: Industry consensus. Same as California; not state specific.
_sources.hardware_deployment_lag_years: Expert prior / scenario assumption. Same value as California; fleet-integration delays are not state specific. Default 2.0 years; epistemic prior U(1, 4) years.
